chunks.text: TEALS EECS Education Day - EECS at Berkeley 2018 CS Education Day participants TEALS EECS Education Day TEALS UC Berkeley’s EECS Education Day is part of the national CS Education Week. This event aims to inspire and motivate high school students to learn more about electrical engineering and computer science, the opportunities this field affords them, and exciting research and educational activities occurring on the UC Berkeley campus. Learn About CS Education Week Tuesday, December 10, 2019 8:30 am to 3:15 pm The goals of TEALS EECS Ed Day are to: Describe the many opportunities for which an education in computer science and electrical engineering prepares K-12 students Provide information and activities for students, educators, parents, and IT professionals to advocate for EECS education at all levels The faculty, students and staff in Berkeley’s computer science community look forward to welcoming a diverse group of participants to learn about the possibilities of computing and to engage in demonstrations, interactive labs, lunch, and more. We do not allow individual registration for this event. Schools are invited. For more information, please contact Heather Levien, heather@berkeley.edu Schedule for December 10, 2019 8:30 AM Arrival & Registration All teachers are responsible for providing transportation for their students. Those coming directly to campus should plan to arrive at 8:30am. Welcome Professor Dan Garcia , ACM Distinguished Educator Kevin Wang , Founder of TEALS (Microsoft’s Technology Education & Literacy in Schools Program ) Dean Lisa Pruitt , Associate Dean for Students, College of Engineering Renaldo Williams
